channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [ORIENTTECH] Promoters of Orient Technologies Ltd Transfer Shares to CEO Mr. Shrihari Kishor Bhat
item.description:
Orient Technologies Ltd announced an off-market transfer of shares by promoters Mr. Ajay Baliram Sawant, Mr. Jayesh Manharlal Shah, Mr. Ujwal Arvind Mhatre, and Mr. Umesh Navnitlal Shah to the CEO of the company, Mr. Shrihari Kishor Bhat. The total voting rights transferred amount to 70.40%. This move is in accordance with Regulation 29(2) under SEBI (SAST) Regulation, 2011.

  • Orient Technologies Ltd promoters transfer shares to CEO Mr. Shrihari Kishor Bhat.
  • Transfer amounts to 70.40% of the total voting rights.
  • Transfer done in accordance with Regulation 29(2) under SEBI (SAST) Regulation, 2011.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Orient Technologies Ltd | Category: Transmission of Shares

item.title: [OLAELEC] Ola Electric Mobility Clarifies on Funding Opportunities and Regulatory Proceedings
item.description:
Ola Electric Mobility Limited has submitted a clarification regarding a news item that appeared in the media. The company is constantly evaluating various opportunities for raising funds at the company and group level, subject to business requirements and market conditions. Any material development in this regard will be disclosed in accordance with applicable laws and regulatory requirements. Ola Electric Mobility also stated that it is not aware of any undisclosed information that could explain the movement in the trading of its equity shares. All material events and information have been duly disclosed to the stock exchanges as required by the SEBI Listing Regulations. There are no material impacts from any regulatory or legal proceedings.

  • Ola Electric Mobility is evaluating various opportunities for raising funds
  • Any material development will be disclosed in accordance with applicable laws and regulatory requirements
  • Ola Electric Mobility is not aware of any undisclosed information that could explain the movement in its equity shares trading
  • All material events and information have been duly disclosed to the stock exchanges as required by the SEBI Listing Regulations
  • There are no material impacts from any regulatory or legal proceedings

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Ola Electric Mobility Ltd | Category: Investment

item.title: [SPICEISL] Spice Islands Industries Ltd Independent Director, Mr. Dipesh Dalvi, Resigns Citing Increased Professional Commitments
item.description:
Spice Islands Industries Ltd has announced the resignation of its Independent Director, Mr. Dipesh Dalvi, due to his increasing professional commitments. The resignation is effective from March 16, 2026. Mr. Dalvi has also stepped down from all the Committees of the Company in which he was a Chairman/Member.

  • Mr. Dipesh Dalvi has resigned from his position as Independent Director of Spice Islands Industries Ltd.
  • The resignation is due to Mr. Dalvi's increasing professional commitments.
  • The resignation is effective from March 16, 2026.
  • Mr. Dalvi has stepped down from all the Committees of the Company in which he was a Chairman/Member.
  • The Company has disclosed all the required information under the Listing Regulations read with the SEBI Master Circular for Compliance with SEBI (LODR) Regulations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SPICE ISLANDS APPARELS LTD.-$ | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [TIL] TIL Ltd Announces Rights Issue of Equity Shares Worth INR 199,51,40,400
item.description:
🔥 Trending

TIL Ltd has fixed the record date for its rights issue on 23rd March, 2026. The company will issue 1,20,91,760 partly paid-up Equity Shares for an amount aggregating up to INR 199,51,40,400. The rights issue price is INR 165 per fully paid-up Equity Share. Eligible Equity Shareholders will receive 11 Rights Equity Shares for every 64 Equity Shares held as on the record date. The company has received in-principle approvals from the National Stock Exchange of India Limited and BSE Limited.

  • TIL Ltd announces a rights issue of equity shares worth INR 199,51,40,400
  • 1,20,91,760 partly paid-up Equity Shares to be issued
  • Rights Issue Price set at INR 165 per fully paid-up Equity Share
  • Record date fixed for determining eligible Equity Shareholders
  • In-principle approvals received from NSE and BSE

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: TIL LTD. | Category: Right Issue

item.title: [TIL] TIL Ltd Announces Rights Issue of Equity Shares
item.description:
🔥 Trending

TIL Ltd, in a meeting held on 16th March, 2026, has approved the proposed issue of equity shares on a rights basis. The rights issue will consist of 1,20,91,760 partly paid-up Equity Shares, amounting to approximately 199,51,40,400/-. The rights entitlement ratio is 11:64, and the record date is fixed as Monday, 234 March, 2026. The company is awaiting final approvals and will re-convene to finalise the Letter of Offer.

  • TIL Ltd approves rights issue of equity shares
  • 1,20,91,760 partly paid-up Equity Shares to be issued
  • Approximately 199,51,40,400/- to be raised
  • Rights entitlement ratio set at 11:64
  • Record date fixed as Monday, 234 March, 2026

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: TIL LTD. | Category: Right Issue

item.title: [BLUECOAST] SEBI Imposes Penalty on Former CFO of Blue Coast Hotels Ltd
item.description:
Pursuant to Regulation 30 read with Schedule III of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, the Adjudicating Officer of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has issued an Adjudication Order dated March 11, 2026, imposing a penalty of Rs. 5,00,000/- on Mr. Amit Kumar Singhl, former Chief Financial Officer of Blue Coast Hotels Ltd. The order was received on March 16, 2026 from National Stock Exchange. The violation of certain provisions of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 read with Section 27 of SEBI Act, 1992 read with Ind AS1,24 and 37 is listed in the order. However, there is no material financial implication for the Company arising out of the said order.

  • SEBI imposes a penalty of Rs. 5,00,000/- on former CFO of Blue Coast Hotels Ltd
  • The order was received on March 16, 2026 from National Stock Exchange
  • The violation of certain provisions of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015 read with Section 27 of SEBI Act, 1992 read with Ind AS1,24 and 37 is listed in the order
  • No material financial implication for the Company arising out of the said order

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BLUE COAST HOTELS LTD. | Category: Legal

item.title: [EDUCOMP] NCLT Directs Fresh Form-G Issuance and CIRP Completion within 100 Days for Educomp Solutions Ltd
item.description:
🔥 Trending

The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) has directed Educomp Solutions Ltd to issue a fresh Form G and complete the Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) within 100 days. This decision came during the hearing held on March 13, 2026. The NCLT also ordered the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) to initiate an investigation into the SRA's failure to implement the approved Resolution Plan. If offences are made out, IBBI has been directed to file a complaint under Section 236 of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016. The disposal of various other Interim Applications includes a Contempt Petition filed by the ERP, a petition filed by SBI for Resolution Plan implementation, a Contempt Petition filed by IDBI Bank, and an application filed by the ERP seeking directions for execution of the Resolution Plan approval.

  • NCLT directs fresh Form-G issuance and CIRP completion within 100 days for Educomp Solutions Ltd
  • IBBI ordered to initiate an investigation into the SRA's failure to implement the approved Resolution Plan
  • NCLT directs IBBI to file a complaint under Section 236 of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 if offences are made out
  • Various Interim Applications disposed of, including Contempt Petitions and execution of Resolution Plan approval

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: EDUCOMP SOLUTIONS LTD. | Category: Legal

item.title: [FUSION] Fusion Finance Ltd. Receives Penalty Order from ROC Delhi for Non-Compliance with Companies Act
item.description:
Fusion Finance Ltd. (Formerly known as Fusion Micro Finance Limited) has received an order dated March 16, 2026 from the Registrar of Companies — ROC, Delhi, for adjudication of a penalty. The penalty was imposed due to the company's non-compliance with Section 152(6) of the Companies Act, 2013, from July 20, 2021 to July 08, 2023. The company was required to have three Non-Independent Directors liable to retire by rotation, whereas only one Non-Independent Director was designated as such during the aforesaid period. The company has been fully compliant with the provisions of Section 152 of the Companies Act, 2013 with effect from July 08, 2023. The penalty imposed on the company is Rs. 3,00,000/- (Rupees Three Lacs Only). A penalty of Rs. 1,00,000/- (Rupees One Lac Only) was also imposed on Mr. Devesh Sachdev, erstwhile Managing Director, being officer in default during the aforesaid period.

  • Fusion Finance Ltd. received an order from ROC Delhi for adjudication of penalty.
  • The penalty was imposed due to non-compliance with Section 152(6) of the Companies Act, 2013.
  • The non-compliance period was from July 20, 2021 to July 08, 2023.
  • The company was required to have three Non-Independent Directors liable to retire by rotation, whereas only one was designated as such.
  • The company has been fully compliant with the provisions of Section 152 of the Companies Act, 2013 with effect from July 08, 2023.
  • The penalty imposed on the company is Rs. 3,00,000/- (Rupees Three Lacs Only).
  • A penalty of Rs. 1,00,000/- (Rupees One Lac Only) was imposed on Mr. Devesh Sachdev, erstwhile Managing Director.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Fusion Micro Finance Ltd | Category: Legal

item.title: [GANESHCP] Ganesh Consumer Products Ltd Announces Resignation of Two Directors
item.description:
Ganesh Consumer Products Ltd has announced the resignation of two directors, Mr. Sunil Rewachand Chandiramani and Mr. Rohit Brijmohan Mantri. Mr. Chandiramani, an Independent Director, has resigned from his position and all committee memberships, including the Audit Committee, Risk Management Committee, and Nomination and Remuneration Committee. Mr. Mantri, a Non-Executive Non-Independent Director, has also resigned from all committee memberships. Both resignations will take effect from the close of business hours on 16th March, 2026. The company has expressed gratitude for their contributions and confirmed that there are no other material reasons for their resignations.

  • Mr. Sunil Rewachand Chandiramani has resigned as Independent Director and from all committee memberships.
  • Mr. Rohit Brijmohan Mantri has resigned as Non-Executive Non-Independent Director and from all committee memberships.
  • Both resignations will take effect from the close of business hours on 16th March, 2026.
  • The company has appreciated the contributions of both directors.
  • There are no other material reasons for the resignations of either director.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Ganesh Consumer Products Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [GANESHCP] Ganesh Consumer Products Ltd Appoints Rajiv Nitin Mehta as Additional Non-Executive Independent Director and Devansh Mimani as Additional Non-Executive Non-Independent Director
item.description:
Ganesh Consumer Products Ltd has appointed Mr. Rajiv Nitin Mehta as an Additional Director (Non-Executive Independent) and Mr. Devansh Mimani as an Additional Director (Non-Executive Non-Independent) at the board meeting held on March 16, 2026. The appointments are subject to the approval of the shareholders. Mr. Mehta has 20+ years of experience in consumer brands, retail, venture capital, and corporate governance, while Mr. Mimani leads marketing and digital initiatives and drives business development through new projects and product innovation.

  • Rajiv Nitin Mehta appointed as Additional Director (Non-Executive Independent)
  • Devansh Mimani appointed as Additional Director (Non-Executive Non-Independent)
  • Mehta has 20+ years of experience in consumer brands, retail, venture capital, and corporate governance
  • Mimani leads marketing and digital initiatives and drives business development through new projects and product innovation
  • Appointments subject to shareholder approval

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Ganesh Consumer Products Ltd | Category: Management Change
